author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
title: No Condemnation in Christ Jesus.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: Then he adds the reason why he said all these things: “O wretched man that I am! who shall deliver me from the body of this death?

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: The grace of God, through Jesus Christ our Lord!”

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: And thence he concludes: “Therefore I myself with the mind serve the law of God, but with the flesh the law of sin.”

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: To wit, with the flesh, the law of sin, by lusting; but with the mind, the law of God, by not consenting to that lust.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: “For there is now no condemnation to those who are in Christ Jesus.”

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: For he is not condemned who does not consent to the evil of the lust of the flesh.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: “For the law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus has made thee free from the law of sin and death,” so that, to wit, the lust of the flesh may not appropriate to itself thy consent.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
verses.text: And what follows more and more demonstrates the same meaning.

verses.book: A Treatise Against Two Letters of the Pelagians.
